title: didymoglossum pygmaeum
intro: Didymoglossum pygmaeum, also known as the pygmy lungwort or antelope's lungwort, is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Didymoglossum pygmaeum include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to the skin as a poultice or salve to aid in wound healing.
